SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.allocation

Class AllocationNetwork

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.allocation.AllocationNetwork
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class AllocationNetwork extends Object implements Serializable
Linear proxy network assembled from frozen per-unit split factors.

The network treats every process unit as a node and every stream as a directed edge. For a given component k, the routing matrix A_k encodes which fraction of each unit's inlet leaves toward every other unit, including recycle / reflux loops. With the split factors frozen from one rigorous base run, the map "component-in → component on each outlet" is linear, so the steady-state inlet flow vector v_k for any set of source injections x_k satisfies:

$$(I - A_k)\,v_k = B_k\,x_k,\qquad y_k = C_k\,v_k$$

where B_k maps source injections onto their entry units and C_k selects custody outlets. Because each unit conserves component mass (the split factors sum to one over outlets) and at least one outlet of the network is a terminal custody stream, the loop gain is strictly below one and (I - A_k) is non-singular — guaranteeing a unique solution by direct solve or convergent fixed-point iteration.

This class only assembles the topology and the per-component A_k matrices plus the source-entry and custody-outlet lookups. The actual linear solve lives in LinearAllocationSolver.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team

  • Constructor Details

    • AllocationNetwork

      public AllocationNetwork(RecoveryFactorExtractor factors)
      Builds an allocation network from extracted recovery factors.
      Parameters:
      factors - the frozen split factors and master slate; must be non-null and already extracted

  • Method Details

    • buildTopology

      private void buildTopology()
      Indexes the nodes and builds identity-based producer / consumer lookups for every stream.

    • buildRoutingMatrix

      public double[][] buildRoutingMatrix(int componentIndex)
      Builds the routing matrix A_k for a single component.

      A[u][w] is the fraction of unit w's component-k inlet that is routed to unit u through the stream(s) connecting them. Terminal outlet streams (custody / product streams not consumed by any node) contribute no row, which is precisely what makes the loop gain less than one.

      Parameters:
      componentIndex - zero-based component index on the master slate
      Returns:
      an n x n routing matrix

    • findEntryUnit

      public int findEntryUnit(StreamInterface feedStream)
      Finds the node index that the given source feed stream enters. A source stream feeds exactly one mixing node in a well-formed flowsheet; if it feeds several, the first is returned.
      Parameters:
      feedStream - the source feed stream; must be non-null
      Returns:
      the entry node index, or -1 if the stream is not connected to any node inlet

    • findProducer

      public int[] findProducer(StreamInterface stream)
      Locates the producing node and outlet index of a custody (product) stream.
      Parameters:
      stream - the custody stream; must be non-null
      Returns:
      a two-element array {unitIndex, outletIndex}, or null if the stream is not produced by any node

    • getCustodyFactor

      public double getCustodyFactor(int[] producer, int componentIndex)
      Gets the split factor of a custody outlet for a component.
      Parameters:
      producer - a {unitIndex, outletIndex} pair from findProducer(StreamInterface)
      componentIndex - zero-based component index on the master slate
      Returns:
      the split factor f_w(s,k)

    • findSourceStreams

      public List<StreamInterface> findSourceStreams()
      Finds candidate source streams: streams that feed a node but are not produced by any node (i.e. external process feeds). Returned in first-seen node order.
      Returns:
      a list of terminal inlet (feed) streams

    • findCustodyStreams

      public List<StreamInterface> findCustodyStreams()
      Finds candidate custody streams: streams produced by a node but not consumed by any node (i.e. terminal product streams). Returned in first-seen node order.
      Returns:
      a list of terminal outlet (product) streams

    • containsIdentity

      private static boolean containsIdentity(List<StreamInterface> list, StreamInterface stream)
      Checks whether a stream is already present in a list by object identity.
      Parameters:
      list - the list to search; must be non-null
      stream - the stream to look for; must be non-null
      Returns:
      true if the same instance is already present

    • getNodeCount

      public int getNodeCount()
      Gets the number of nodes in the network.
      Returns:
      the node count

    • getComponentCount

      public int getComponentCount()
      Gets the number of components on the master slate.
      Returns:
      the component count

    • getFactors

      public RecoveryFactorExtractor getFactors()
      Gets the underlying recovery-factor extractor.
      Returns:
      the extractor

    • getUnits

      public List<ProcessEquipmentInterface> getUnits()
      Gets the node units in network order.
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable list of node units
